differential_diagnoses: Common variable immunodeficiency (CVID) with associated enteropathy
Infectious enteritis (e.g., refractory Giardiasis, Whipple's disease)
next_steps: Surgical consultation for possible exploratory laparotomy or resection of necrotic/ischemic jejunal segments
Repeat diagnostic imaging (CT enterography or MRE) to evaluate for interval progression of bowel wall ischemia/aneurysmal dilatation
Bone marrow biopsy and aspirate to investigate the clonal NK-cell population and potential underlying lymphoproliferative disorder
Endoscopic or surgical tissue biopsy of the jejunal segments for definitive histopathology and immunohistochemistry (T-cell markers, EBER-ISH)
Assessment for secondary causes of immune deficiency beyond CVID, such as specific T-cell subsets and lymphocyte transformation tests
Transfusion management and hemodynamic monitoring given the persistent anemia and clinical concern for ischemia

differential_diagnoses: Indolent T-cell lymphoproliferative disorder of the gastrointestinal tract
Common variable immunodeficiency (CVID) with lymphoproliferative complications
Crohn's disease with secondary aneurysmal dilatation
next_steps: Surgical consultation for urgent resection of symptomatic/ischemic jejunal segments
Pathological examination of resected tissue with T-cell receptor (TCR) gene rearrangement studies and immunohistochemistry (CD3, CD4, CD8, CD56, EBER-ISH)
Flow cytometry of blood and biopsy tissue for clonal NK-cell and T-cell populations
Bone marrow aspiration and biopsy for staging and assessment of systemic lymphoproliferative involvement
Transfusion of packed red blood cells to stabilize hemoglobin for surgical safety
Repeat imaging (CT angiography) to monitor for acute progression of mural ischemia
Nutritional optimization and bridging with total parenteral nutrition
